title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will return to play against the Washington Wizards after resting as part of injury management.
Tatum is under a minutes restriction after returning from a right Achilles injury suffered 10 months ago.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable, while Nikola Vucevic is out for the game against the Wizards.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix's 'War Machine' features Alan Ritchson as a soldier excelling in Army Ranger training amidst a looming threat.
Disney+'s 'Zootopia 2' follows Nick and Judy as they face a new mystery while struggling as police partners.
HBO Max's 'Rooster' stars Steve Carell as an author navigating life on a college campus after his divorce.

title: 5 things to know about Patriots offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots signed former Jets offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker to bolster their line after trading center Garrett Bradbury.
Vera-Tucker, 26, has started 43 games in the NFL but missed last season due to a triceps injury.
He was persuaded to join the Patriots by Morgan Moses, his former teammate with the Jets, now New England's right tackle.

title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora named Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox team.
Trevor Story played 157 games in 2025 and led the Red Sox in home runs, RBI, and runs.
Garrett Crochet led MLB in strikeouts with 255 and was Boston's Opening Day starter in 2025.

title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC will debut in NWSL against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium on March 14, 2026.
Head coach Filipa Patão aims for a possession-style game despite challenges from Gotham's strong roster.
The match marks the first use of new grass at Gillette Stadium, anticipating better play conditions for Legacy FC.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers are debating bill S.2157 to end the biannual clock changes and adopt a permanent time system.
Senator John F. Keenan supports the bill, citing health benefits like reduced heart attacks and improved workplace productivity.
Research links clock changes to health risks; however, ending them may affect daylight distribution, important for New Englanders.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People announced the 2026 Pride celebration will be held on Saturday, June 6.
The parade will start at 11 a.m. in Copley Square and end at Boston Common with a festival.
Mayor Michelle Wu praised the organization for promoting joy and inclusivity in its diverse LGBTQ+ events.

title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 14-year-old Brazilian girl detained by ICE in Marlborough was released by a federal judge and returned to Massachusetts.
She was initially detained due to suspicions of being with gang affiliates, according to a DHS spokesperson.
The girl received a notice to appear in immigration court, indicating potential deportation proceedings, per her lawyer.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins struggle in scoring with top players like David Pastrnak underperforming post-Olympic break.
Boston holds the final Eastern Conference wild-card spot by one point entering a crucial road trip.
Coach Marco Sturm considers reshuffling forward lines, including separating Pastrnak and Lindholm, to reignite offense.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Swiss researcher Alexis Cogne is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise $10,000 for the Stepping Strong Center.
Cogne, a 26-year-old from Geneva, Switzerland, is a visiting researcher in neurorehabilitation at Harvard Medical School.
Funds raised will support trauma research, rehabilitation, and patient programs at Brigham and Women's Hospital.
